summarization.a: The figure shows a contour heatmap of Kerr rotation angle (θKerr) in millidegrees for oxygen deficient ZnO.
summarization.b: The figure shows a contour heatmap of Kerr rotation angle (θKerr) in millidegrees for ZnO:Fe samples.

a.question: Is only  a clockwise Kerr rotation (i.e., positive +θKerr values)  observed for undoped ZnO?
How inhomogenoeus are the θKerr values for undoped ZnO?
a.answer:
Undoped ZnO layers are homogeneous, and the values of θKerr are very small, most of the data are in the range [0−20]
mdeg.

b.question: How does the Fe-doped ZnO Kerr angle map differ from undoped ZnO?
b.answer: The ZnO/Fe layer demonstrates inhomogeneous distribution of θKerr whose values are significantly (2 orders of magnitude) larger than those of the undoped layer.

b.question: What does the ZnO:Fe Kerr angle map reveal?
b.answer: An interesting feature is that both clockwise (positive θKerr) and counterclockwise (negative θKerr) Kerr polarization rotations are observed in the Kerr angle map of Fe-doped ZnO, indicative of complex MO process activity
